description_long: This is a single-story house with a prominent terracotta-tiled gable roof and a rectangular footprint. The front facade features a covered porch supported by columns, leading to a central wooden door flanked by windows. The exterior walls are finished in a light, neutral color, contrasting with the warm roof tiles. The house has a simple, clean design with minimal ornamentation, and the windows and doors are symmetrically arranged on the front. The side and rear elevations are mostly solid, with few or no windows, emphasizing privacy. The structure sits on a flat, paved base, suggesting a suburban or residential neighborhood setting.
description: A single-story house with a terracotta gable roof, covered front porch, and light-colored exterior walls. The design is simple and symmetrical, with a central entryway and minimal window openings.
description_view_0: Front view showing the covered porch, central door, and symmetrical windows beneath the terracotta roof.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the solid wall, gable roof profile, and open porch area on the left.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying a mostly solid wall with minimal openings and the full expanse of the roof.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the plain wall and the gable roof's slope, with a glimpse of the porch at the far end.
attribute_window_arrangement: symmetrical placement on front, minimal on sides and rear
attribute_entryway_design: Central wooden door under a covered porch with columns and a flat paved approach
